message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate whether the acceptance harness is now a TRUSTWORTHY governing oracle. Write /tmp/harness_gate.md then run 'cd /tmp && timeout 300 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/harness_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -40' (bubblewrap warning fine; no sandbox flags).
Give codex:
- The 3 prior blockers: (1) assertion-vacuous THIN positive control, (2) non-deterministic live calls / no frozen trace, (3) not runnable-to-completion (no browser).
- Positive-control fix: {"assertion_code":"valid_positive_control = search_calls >= 1 and outline_mutated
print(f"valid_positive_control={valid_positive_control} "
      f"(search_calls>=1={search_calls >= 1} [search_calls={search_calls}], "
      f"outline_mutated={outline_mutated})")

# --- returned in run_thin()'s dict (new key) ---
"valid_positive_control": valid_positive_control,

# --- main() non-zero exit gate (after writing acceptance_result.json) ---
failures: list[str] = []
if "thin" in result and not result["thin"].get("valid_positive_control"):
    failures.append("positive control (THIN): valid_positive_control=False")
if "saturated" in result and not result["saturated"].get("valid_negative_control"):
    failures.append("negative control (SATURATED): valid_negative_control=False")
if failures:
    print("\nACCEPTANCE FAILED:")
    for f in failures:
        print(f"  - {f}")
    sys.exit(1)
print("\nACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid")","line_range":"run_thin(): valid_positive_control computed at lines 149-155 and returned at line 166 of /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py; main() non-zero exit gate at lines 329-343 (sys.exit(1) at line 342).","exit_nonzero_wired":true}
- Feasibility finding: {"feasible_browser_free":true,"plan_or_blocker":"test","seed_artifact":"test","retrieval_seam":"test","details":"test"}
- Implementation result: {"deterministic_replay_proven":true,"golden_sha256":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","cassette_path":"/home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_llm.jsonl (LLM boundary) and /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_retrieval.jsonl (retrieval/browser boundary); golden artifact at /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json","positive_control_pass":true,"negative_control_pass":true,"notes":"DETERMINISTIC BROWSER-FREE GOLDEN IMPLEMENTED AND PROVEN.

Golden SHA-256: 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 — byte-identical across the recorded seed run + 3 independent REPLAY runs (A, B, and a C run taken AFTER I removed all debug instrumentation from the target code, which proves the debug was inert and the oracle holds). Each replay does ZERO browser/network activity (grep for chrome-launch/Serper/fetch/[ACCESS] = 0 matches).

CONTROLS (both PASS in every replay):
- THIN positive control: valid_positive_control=True (search_more_evidence fired 3 times >=1, outline_mutated=True).
- SATURATED negative control: valid_negative_control=True (zero searches, full loop ran: 3 sections, checklist_ran, finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn 1).

WHAT WAS BUILT (all changes confined to tests/oracle/*; the governing target src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py is UNCHANGED — git diff of it is empty):
1. tests/oracle/retrieval_cassette.py (NEW): the retrieval seam. Monkeypatches live_retriever.run_live_retrieval (the single seam both the harness bootstrap AND the agent's search_more_evidence tool funnel through). Record uses the network once (the seed), replay reconstructs a frozen LiveRetrievalResult with no browser. Sweeps sys.modules to also rebind the harness's __main__ copy of the name. Retrieval caps (max_serper/max_s2/fetch_cap) are EXCLUDED from the cassette key — they are an env-driven cost dial that legitimately differs record-vs-replay and do not select behaviour.
2. tests/oracle/llm_cassette.py (EXTENDED): now freezes BOTH OpenRouterClient.generate AND generate_structured. The decide step uses generate_structured (parsed ReactDecision) — leaving it un-frozen was the core determinism defect (live decide -> divergent loop control flow).
3. tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py (EXTENDED): --seed-retrieval | --record | --replay modes; _frozen_step_clock normalizes the wall-clock elapsed the notebook bakes into the decide prompt AT SOURCE (both the step-header elapsed and the search ToolResult.markdown 'in {N}s', before the length-sensitive digest truncation — the off-by-one that made the decide prompt differ by a single char); canonical golden emission (drops elapsed_s, normalizes in-disclosure timings) + SHA-256 byte-compare in replay.

RUN COMMANDS (env pins the loop turn budget so record==replay; caps keep flaky-source fetching under the wall):
- Seed (records both cassettes + golden, uses live OpenRouter + live retrieval ONCE):
  cd /home/polaris/wt/phase0 && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP=10 PG_FETCH_DEADLINE_SECONDS=20 PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT=10 PG_LIVE_MAX_SERPER=8 PG_LIVE_MAX_S2=8 /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --seed-retrieval
- Replay (frozen LLM + frozen retrieval, no network):
  cd /home/polaris/wt/phase0 && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --replay

OpenRouter recording SUCCEEDED (live z-ai/glm-5.2, no rate-limit/credential failure); .env at /workspace/POLARIS/.env. No cassette was faked.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE / plan interpretation: the plan text and seed artifact were literal 'test' placeholders, so I designed the concrete seam. Because the in-loop gap query is LLM-derived (temperature 0.2), retrieval cannot be frozen independently of the LLM without drift; therefore the golden is recorded by the seed run itself (records LLM + retrieval together, guaranteeing consistency) and REPLAY proves determinism — this maps the plan's step-2 RECORD onto --seed-retrieval and step-3 REPLAY onto --replay. Getting to byte-identity required fixing four real non-determinism leaks discovered by the oracle's own fail-loud MISS/mismatch signals: (a) generate_structured not frozen; (b) per-step and search-markdown wall-clock elapsed leaking into the decide prompt; (c) env-driven fetch_cap in the retrieval key; (d) the harness bootstrap seam patching the wrong (dotted vs __main__) module copy."}
Ask: "Are all three oracle-trustworthiness blockers now resolved? Specifically: does the THIN positive control now enforceably fail on 0 searches, and is the harness now deterministic (byte-identical golden across replays) and runnable-to-completion browser-free? If the golden was seeded from an existing artifact rather than a fresh live browser run, is that acceptable for a REFACTOR-SAFETY oracle (which must detect code changes, not reproduce the original crawl)? Name any residual gap. End with: ORACLE-TRUSTWORTHY or ORACLE-STILL-BLOCKED, and if blocked, the single blocking item."
Return codex's verdict + points verbatim.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate prompt for CODEX and run it.

input.content:
You are a rigorous acceptance-oracle auditor. Decide whether an "acceptance harness" is now a TRUSTWORTHY governing oracle for a REFACTOR-SAFETY use case (its job is to detect code changes / regressions in an outline-generation agent, NOT to reproduce an original web crawl).

## Background: the 3 prior blockers that made it UNTRUSTWORTHY

1. **Assertion-vacuous THIN positive control.** The THIN scenario is meant to prove that when evidence is thin, the agent actually searches for more and mutates its outline. Previously the "positive control" computed nothing enforceable — it could pass with 0 searches. A positive control that cannot fail is worthless.

2. **Non-deterministic live calls / no frozen trace.** The harness made live LLM (OpenRouter) and live retrieval/browser calls. Both the `generate`/`generate_structured` LLM boundary and the retrieval boundary were live. Wall-clock elapsed times leaked into the LLM decide prompt. Result: no byte-stable golden, non-reproducible, cannot govern.

3. **Not runnable-to-completion (no browser).** The harness required a live browser/crawl to run end-to-end, so it could not be executed in a clean/CI environment.

## Fix 1 — Positive-control enforcement (claimed)

```
valid_positive_control = search_calls >= 1 and outline_mutated
print(f"valid_positive_control={valid_positive_control} "
      f"(search_calls>=1={search_calls >= 1} [search_calls={search_calls}], "
      f"outline_mutated={outline_mutated})")

# --- returned in run_thin()'s dict (new key) ---
"valid_positive_control": valid_positive_control,

# --- main() non-zero exit gate (after writing acceptance_result.json) ---
failures: list[str] = []
if "thin" in result and not result["thin"].get("valid_positive_control"):
    failures.append("positive control (THIN): valid_positive_control=False")
if "saturated" in result and not result["saturated"].get("valid_negative_control"):
    failures.append("negative control (SATURATED): valid_negative_control=False")
if failures:
    print("\nACCEPTANCE FAILED:")
    for f in failures:
        print(f"  - {f}")
    sys.exit(1)
print("\nACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid")
```

Location: `run_thin()` computes `valid_positive_control` at lines 149-155 and returns it at line 166 of `/home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py`; `main()` non-zero exit gate at lines 329-343 (`sys.exit(1)` at line 342). `exit_nonzero_wired = true`.

Note: `valid_positive_control = (search_calls >= 1) and outline_mutated`. On 0 searches, `search_calls >= 1` is False, so `valid_positive_control` is False, which appends a failure and calls `sys.exit(1)`. Assess whether this now enforceably fails on 0 searches.

## Fix 2 + 3 — Deterministic, browser-free golden (claimed implementation result)

- `deterministic_replay_proven = true`
- Golden SHA-256: `9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98` — claimed byte-identical across the recorded seed run + 3 independent REPLAY runs (A, B, and a C run taken AFTER removing all debug instrumentation from the target code, proving the debug was inert).
- Each replay claimed to do ZERO browser/network activity (grep for chrome-launch/Serper/fetch/[ACCESS] = 0 matches).
- Cassettes: `/home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_llm.jsonl` (LLM boundary) and `acceptance_retrieval.jsonl` (retrieval/browser boundary); golden at `acceptance_golden.json`.
- Controls both PASS in every replay: THIN `valid_positive_control=True` (search_more_evidence fired 3 times >=1, outline_mutated=True); SATURATED `valid_negative_control=True` (zero searches, full loop ran: 3 sections, checklist_ran, finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn 1).
- All changes confined to `tests/oracle/*`; the governing target `src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py` is UNCHANGED (empty git diff).
- New `tests/oracle/retrieval_cassette.py` monkeypatches `live_retriever.run_live_retrieval` (single seam both the harness bootstrap AND the agent's `search_more_evidence` tool funnel through). Retrieval caps (max_serper/max_s2/fetch_cap) EXCLUDED from cassette key (env-driven cost dial, not behaviour-selecting).
- Extended `tests/oracle/llm_cassette.py` now freezes BOTH `OpenRouterClient.generate` AND `generate_structured` (the decide step uses generate_structured — leaving it un-frozen was the core determinism defect).
- `_frozen_step_clock` normalizes wall-clock elapsed baked into the decide prompt AT SOURCE (both step-header elapsed and search ToolResult.markdown "in {N}s") before the length-sensitive digest truncation.
- Canonical golden emission drops elapsed_s, normalizes in-disclosure timings, then SHA-256 byte-compares in replay.

### IMPORTANT interpretation note from the implementer (assess this honestly)

The plan text and seed artifact were literal `"test"` placeholders, so the implementer designed the concrete seam themselves. Because the in-loop gap query is LLM-derived (temperature 0.2), retrieval cannot be frozen independently of the LLM without drift; therefore the golden is recorded by the SEED run itself (records LLM + retrieval together, guaranteeing consistency), and REPLAY proves determinism. This maps the plan's step-2 RECORD onto `--seed-retrieval` and step-3 REPLAY onto `--replay`.

The feasibility finding fields were also placeholders: `{"feasible_browser_free":true, "plan_or_blocker":"test", "seed_artifact":"test", "retrieval_seam":"test", "details":"test"}`.

Byte-identity required fixing four real non-determinism leaks the oracle's own fail-loud MISS/mismatch signals surfaced: (a) generate_structured not frozen; (b) per-step and search-markdown wall-clock elapsed leaking into decide prompt; (c) env-driven fetch_cap in retrieval key; (d) harness bootstrap seam patching the wrong (dotted vs __main__) module copy.

## Your task

Answer these, then render a verdict:

1. Is blocker #1 resolved — does the THIN positive control now ENFORCEABLY fail on 0 searches (traces to a non-zero process exit)?
2. Is blocker #2 resolved — is the harness now deterministic (byte-identical golden across replays)?
3. Is blocker #3 resolved — is it runnable-to-completion browser-free?
4. The golden was seeded from the SEED run (records LLM+retrieval together) rather than a fresh independent live browser crawl. For a REFACTOR-SAFETY oracle (which must detect code changes, not reproduce the original crawl), is that acceptable? Is there any circularity risk (e.g., the golden encodes agent behaviour that a refactor is supposed to be free to change, causing false regressions; or the oracle only exercises the frozen path and would miss a real code change)?
5. Name any residual gap.

End your answer with exactly one of these two tokens on its own line: `ORACLE-TRUSTWORTHY` or `ORACLE-STILL-BLOCKED`. If blocked, immediately after the token give the SINGLE blocking item in one sentence.

    checklist_gaps = sum(

    )
    # Enforceable POSITIVE control (mirrors run_saturated's valid_negative_control): the thin case
    # MUST have fired at least one real scoped retrieval AND mutated the outline vs the seed. If
    # either is false the harness is asserting nothing and the control is vacuous — fail loud.
    valid_positive_control = search_calls >= 1 and outline_mutated
    print(f"valid_positive_control={valid_positive_control} "
          f"(search_calls>=1={search_calls >= 1} [search_calls={search_calls}], "
          f"outline_mutated={outline_mutated})")
    return {
        "elapsed_s": round(elapsed, 1),
        "turns": stats.get("turns"),
        "ev_before": len(ev_before),
        "ev_after": stats.get("ev_store_size"),

        "final_section_ev_counts": {
            oa._plan_field(p, "title", ""): len(oa._plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])  # noqa: SLF001

    if "--mode" in sys.argv:
        idx = sys.argv.index("--mode")
        if idx + 1 < len(sys.argv):
            return sys.argv[idx + 1].strip().lower()
    return None


import re as _re  # noqa: E402

# Wall-clock timings the outline agent bakes into disclosure strings (outline_agent.py:821 emits a
# per-search ``... in {elapsed:.1f}s``). These are the ONE non-deterministic substring inside the
# otherwise pure ``disclosures`` list — frozen retrieval returns instantly on replay, so the real
# fetch elapsed differs from the recorded one. Normalizing it to a fixed token keeps the golden a
# function of BEHAVIOUR (what searched/folded/mutated), not of fetch latency.
_ELAPSED_IN_DISCLOSURE = _re.compile(r" in \d+\.\d+s\b")


def _normalize_disclosure(s: str) -> str:
    return _ELAPSED_IN_DISCLOSURE.sub(" in <elapsed>s", s)

input.description: Run codex gate on harness trustworthiness
